name: Recover incomplete checkouts with HTTP / REST API and Microsoft Teams on autopilot

parameters.message: =Quick tip for {{ $json.company || 'your team' }} — see what others automate.

parameters.text: =✅ Recover incomplete checkouts with HTTP / REST API and Microsoft Teams  — run completed at {{ $now.toFormat('HH:mm') }}

parameters.content:
### Weekly Schedule

Starts the workflow on a timer — weekly. No input data; downstream nodes fetch what they need.

Next: Keep Valid Orders

### Keep Valid Orders

Drops items that fail the condition; only qualifying items continue (no false branch).

Next: Segment Audience

### Segment Audience

Two-way gate on `={{ ($json.score ?? 50) >= 50 }}`. TRUE continues the happy path, FALSE takes the alternate branch.

Ports: ①→ Step 1 — Welcome (Microsoft Teams) · ②→ Tag for Slow Nurture

### Step 1 — Welcome (Microsoft Teams)

Posts a chat message into a Microsoft Teams channel — same role as a Slack notify, for teams standardized on Teams.

Ports: ①→ Wait Before Step 2 · ②→ Capture Failure Context

↻ retries 3×/2s · ⚠ failures exit via the error rail · 🔑 Microsoft OAuth2

### Tag for Slow Nurture

Upserts a row into 'Order Tracker', matched on `orderId` — re-running with the same key updates the row instead of duplicating it.

↻ retries 3×/2s · ⚠ failures exit via the error rail · 🔑 Google OAuth2 / service account

### Wait Before Step 2

Pauses 2 days — spacing calls to respect rate limits.

Next: Converted Already?

### Converted Already?

Two-way gate on `={{ $json.status }}`. TRUE continues the happy path, FALSE takes the alternate branch.

Ports: ①→ Step 2 — Value Nudge (Microsoft Teams) · ②→ Mark Converted — Stop Drip

### Step 2 — Value Nudge (Microsoft Teams)

Posts a chat message into a Microsoft Teams channel — same role as a Slack notify, for teams standardized on Teams.

Ports: ①→ Update Drip Status · ②→ Capture Failure Context

↻ retries 3×/2s · ⚠ failures exit via the error rail · 🔑 Microsoft OAuth2

### Mark Converted — Stop Drip

Upserts a row into 'Order Tracker', matched on `orderId` — re-running with the same key updates the row instead of duplicating it.

↻ retries 3×/2s · ⚠ failures exit via the error rail · 🔑 Google OAuth2 / service account

### Update Drip Status

Upserts a row into 'Order Tracker', matched on `orderId` — re-running with the same key updates the row instead of duplicating it.

Ports: ①→ Guard Non-Empty email · ②→ Capture Failure Context

↻ retries 3×/2s · ⚠ failures exit via the error rail · 🔑 Google OAuth2 / service account

### Guard Non-Empty email

Drops items that fail the condition; only qualifying items continue (no false branch).

Next: Summarize Run Stats

### Summarize Run Stats

Shapes the item: writes `runAt`, `itemCount`, `status`. Keeps the schema explicit between stages.

Next: Append Audit Row (Google Sheets)

### Append Audit Row (Google Sheets)

Appends a new row to 'Order Tracker' — a human-readable audit trail that doesn't require opening n8n to inspect.

Ports: ①→ Send Summary to Slack · ②→ Capture Failure Context

↻ retries 3×/2s · ⚠ failures exit via the error rail · 🔑 Google OAuth2 / service account

### Send Summary to Slack

Posts a message to Slack (`#orders`) — a low-latency way for the team to see automation activity without checking a dashboard.

↻ retries 3×/2s · ⚠ failures exit via the error rail · 🔑 Slack OAuth2

### Capture Failure Context

Shapes the item: writes `failedNode`, `errorMessage`, `workflow`. Keeps the schema explicit between stages.

Next: Escalate Failure to Microsoft Teams

### Escalate Failure to Microsoft Teams

Posts a chat message into a Microsoft Teams channel — same role as a Slack notify, for teams standardized on Teams.

↻ retries 3×/2s · 🔑 Microsoft OAuth2

nodes.name: Note: Escalate Failure to Microsoft Teams

parameters.content:
# Recover incomplete checkouts with HTTP / REST API and Microsoft Teams on autopilot

**Pattern:** Abandoned Cart Recovery · **Level:** Advanced · **Trigger:** Scheduled

## What it does
Recover incomplete checkouts with HTTP / REST API and Microsoft Teams on autopilot. It runs automatically on a schedule.

## Why it exists
Manual cart recovery is slow and error-prone; this workflow makes the process repeatable, monitored and hands-off, with failures surfaced instead of silently lost.

## Main flow
Weekly Schedule → Keep Valid Orders → Segment Audience → Step 1 — Welcome (Microsoft Teams) → Wait Before Step 2 → Converted Already? → Step 2 — Value Nudge (Microsoft Teams) → …

## Use it for
- Teams that want to abandoned cart recovery without manual effort
- Anyone automating cart recovery in e-commerce
- Replacing a fragile manual process with a reliable workflow

## You get
- Scales without extra headcount
- Improves response time
- Has retries and error handling built in

## Before first run
Connect credentials: Google OAuth2 / service account; Microsoft OAuth2; Slack OAuth2.
Follow the color key: 🟦 entry · 🟪 AI · 🟫 actions · ⬜ utilities · 🟩 outputs · 🟥 error rail.
Each node has its own doc card underneath — read those before editing.

## Try it now
Sample data is pinned on the trigger — hit **Execute Workflow** to watch it flow through every node before connecting any real credentials. Unpin the trigger (right-click → Unpin) when you're ready to go live.

## Design decisions

Every step checks 'Converted Already?' before sending — a drip campaign that keeps emailing someone who already bought is the fastest way to burn trust. The Wait node's durable resume state (via `webhookId`) is what lets a multi-day wait survive an n8n restart instead of being held only in memory.

## Practice exercises

You've got the basics covered — push further:

1. Add a 4th drip step with its own wait/exit-check pair.
2. Manually flip a test record's status to 'converted' mid-drip and confirm the ladder stops sending.
3. Shorten the wait durations so the full ladder runs in minutes instead of days, for testing.

## 🛟 Error handling & retries

External calls retry **3× with 2s backoff**. Anything still failing exits through its node's error output into the red error rail → context is captured and the on-call channel is alerted. Set an n8n **Error Workflow** in settings as the final safety net.

## ⏱ Rate limiting

Work is batched and spaced with Wait so provider quotas aren't tripped. If you hit 429s, lower the batch size or raise the wait — throughput drops, reliability rises.

## 🛠 Customize me

Quick wins: point the data nodes at your own accounts (see 🔑 lines on each card), adjust the trigger cadence, and tune thresholds/labels to your cart recovery process. Every node card explains its role — safe to extend at any marked branch.
